questions.q: Who wrote the song 'Vande Mataram'?
questions.explanation: Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay composed 'Vande Mataram'.

questions.q: From which novel by Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay does 'Vande Mataram' come?
questions.explanation: 'Vande Mataram' appears in Bankim Chandra's novel Anandamath.

questions.q: What does the phrase 'Vande Mataram' broadly mean?
questions.explanation: 'Vande Mataram' means 'I bow to thee, Mother', personifying the motherland.

questions.q: 'Vande Mataram' was adopted as what for India?
questions.explanation: 'Vande Mataram' was adopted as the national song of India, distinct from the national anthem.

questions.q: 'Vande Mataram' became a rallying cry during which movement?
questions.explanation: 'Vande Mataram' became a rallying cry during the Swadeshi era following the 1905 Partition of Bengal.

questions.q: In which language was 'Vande Mataram' originally composed?
options: A mix of Sanskrit and Bengali
questions.explanation: The song was composed in a Sanskritized Bengali, blending Sanskrit and Bengali.

questions.q: 'Vande Mataram' personifies the nation as which figure?
questions.explanation: The song hails the motherland personified as the Mother goddess.

questions.q: Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay, who wrote 'Vande Mataram', was from which region of India?
questions.explanation: Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay was a Bengali writer.

questions.q: Singing 'Vande Mataram' during the freedom struggle was often seen by the British as an act of what?
questions.explanation: Authorities often treated the singing of 'Vande Mataram' as an act of nationalist political defiance.

questions.q: 'Vande Mataram' is distinct from which of these, the national anthem of India?
questions.explanation: India's national anthem is 'Jana Gana Mana', while 'Vande Mataram' is the national song.

questions.q: In which year was the novel Anandamath, containing 'Vande Mataram', first published?
questions.explanation: Anandamath was published in 1882, though the song itself was written a few years earlier.

questions.q: At which 1896 Congress session is 'Vande Mataram' said to have been first sung by Rabindranath Tagore?
questions.explanation: Rabindranath Tagore is widely credited with first singing 'Vande Mataram' at the 1896 Calcutta Congress session.

questions.q: Anandamath is set against the backdrop of which historical event in Bengal?
questions.explanation: Anandamath is set against the eighteenth-century Sannyasi Rebellion in Bengal.

questions.q: In 1937, which Congress committee was tasked with examining the status of 'Vande Mataram' as a national song?
options: A committee involving Nehru and Tagore
questions.explanation: In 1937 the Congress, with input from Nehru and Tagore, decided to adopt the first two stanzas as the national song.

questions.q: Which two stanzas of 'Vande Mataram' were formally adopted to address concerns over later verses?
questions.explanation: The first two stanzas, seen as a hymn to the land, were adopted as the national song.

questions.q: On which date in 1950 did the Constituent Assembly formally accord 'Vande Mataram' equal honour with the national anthem?
questions.explanation: On 24 January 1950 the Constituent Assembly resolved that 'Vande Mataram' would have equal status with 'Jana Gana Mana'.

questions.q: Who is credited with giving 'Vande Mataram' a famous musical setting in the 1900s?
questions.explanation: Rabindranath Tagore is credited with setting an early and influential tune to 'Vande Mataram'.

questions.q: The cry 'Vande Mataram' was prominently used at protest meetings against which 1905 colonial measure?
questions.explanation: Protesters chanted 'Vande Mataram' at meetings against the 1905 Partition of Bengal.

questions.q: An English-language nationalist newspaper of the Swadeshi era was named after which phrase?
questions.explanation: The newspaper Bande Mataram, linked to Aurobindo Ghosh, took its name from the famous song.

questions.q: Some objections to the full song arose because later stanzas identify the motherland with which goddess imagery?
questions.explanation: Later stanzas identify the motherland with Hindu goddesses such as Durga, prompting the adoption of only the first two stanzas.

questions.q: Revolutionaries during the freedom struggle often used 'Vande Mataram' as what?
questions.explanation: 'Vande Mataram' served as a slogan and battle cry for nationalists and revolutionaries alike.

questions.q: Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay served the colonial administration in which capacity during his lifetime?
questions.explanation: Bankim Chandra worked as a deputy magistrate and deputy collector in the British administration.

questions.q: In approximately which year did Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay actually write the verses of 'Vande Mataram', before inserting them in Anandamath?
questions.explanation: Bankim is generally said to have written the verses around 1875, later weaving them into Anandamath (1882).

questions.q: Which artist is associated with the celebrated 1905 painting of 'Bharat Mata' that resonated with the spirit of 'Vande Mataram'?
questions.explanation: Abanindranath Tagore's early 'Bharat Mata' painting visually echoed the motherland imagery of 'Vande Mataram'.

questions.q: In which decade did A. R. Rahman release his hugely popular non-film album titled 'Vande Mataram'?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman released his celebrated album 'Vande Mataram', including the song 'Maa Tujhe Salaam', in 1997 to mark fifty years of Indian independence.

questions.q: Which revolutionary, executed young, is said to have walked to the gallows with 'Vande Mataram' on his lips during the freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Khudiram Bose, hanged in 1908 at a very young age, is famously remembered for invoking 'Vande Mataram'.

questions.q: The 1937 Congress decision on 'Vande Mataram' was significantly shaped by a note written by which leader who consulted Tagore?
questions.explanation: Jawaharlal Nehru, consulting Rabindranath Tagore, helped frame the 1937 resolution adopting the first two stanzas.

questions.q: In its 1950 statement, the Constituent Assembly president who pronounced 'Vande Mataram's equal honour was whom?
questions.explanation: Constituent Assembly president Rajendra Prasad made the 24 January 1950 statement granting 'Vande Mataram' equal honour.

questions.q: The song 'Vande Mataram' was first published as part of Anandamath in which Bengali literary magazine edited by Bankim?
questions.explanation: Anandamath was first serialized in Bangadarshan, the literary magazine edited by Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay.

questions.q: A widely admired English translation of 'Vande Mataram' was rendered by which nationalist thinker?
questions.explanation: Aurobindo Ghosh produced a celebrated English translation of 'Vande Mataram'.
